events.description: Join us (rain or shine) for the annual LakeApalooza Green Lake Community Street Dance on Saturday, September 5! All the fun begins at 5:00 pm downtown Green Lake by the American Legion. Live music by Davidson County Band starts at 7:00 pm. Come hungry, as there will be many food choices. A full bar will be at the American Legion Post 306. Let’s end our Green Lake summer with a bang and enjoy it together in downtown Green Lake. Hope to see you there! Attendees may bring their own chairs This fantastic event is brought to you by Mike & Jill Havey Please direct questions regarding this event to the Green Lake Chamber of Commerce: 920.294.3231 or info@visitgreenlake.com

events.title: Taste & Tunes | Green Lake Wine Walk
events.location: Downtown Green Lake
events.venue_name: Downtown Green Lake
events.description: Dust off those leg warmers, tease up that hair, and get ready to party like it's the '80s! 🪩🍷🎶 The Green Lake Area Chamber of Commerce is excited to host the Fourth Annual Taste & Tunes Wine Walk on Friday, August 7, 2026, from 4:30 PM – 8:00 PM in Downtown Green Lake. Neon everywhere, live tunes, great wine, and big hair are taking over Downtown Green Lake for one unforgettable evening. Participating businesses will be serving a variety of unique wine samples while embracing this year's all-out '80s theme. Stop locations and entertainment details will be announced closer to the event. 🎟️ Tickets go on sale Thursday, June 11 at 12:00 PM. Your ticket includes: 🍷 A variety of unique wine samples at participating stops 🥤 A custom Green Lake Wine Walk tumbler, sponsored by Solara Aevum Wellness & Aesthetics 👜 The first 70 registrations will receive a FREE 2025 Wine Walk tote bag Throw on your best '80s attire, call up your friends, and get ready to stroll from stop to stop as our decked-out businesses bring the decade to life. From unique wine samples and live tunes to shopping and plenty of throwback fun, it's sure to be an unforgettable summer evening in Downtown Green Lake. Must be 21 years of age or older to attend. Tickets are non-refundable.

events.title: GLA 13dot1 Races
events.location: Town Square Terrace
events.venue_name: Town Square Terrace
events.description: Green Lake Area 13dot1 Races – Half Marathon, 10K & 5K Kick off Labor Day Weekend in scenic Green Lake with your choice of a Half Marathon, 10K, or 5K —all starting and finishing at the Town Square Community Center Terrace. Our race courses wind through historic downtown, past local shops and parks, and along the breathtaking shoreline of Big Green Lake. Runners will enjoy a mix of charming neighborhoods, lakefront vistas, and wooded pathways, with the Half Marathon featuring an extended tour through the beautiful Green Lake Conference Center grounds. All routes offer family-friendly fun, community spirit, and memorable views, ending back at Town Square for post-race celebrations.

events.description: The Wisconsin State Chili Cook-Off is the only sanctioned chili cook-off in Wisconsin . events.title: Glass Art Workshop with Susan Melgaard
events.location:
Town Square Community Center MakerSpace
492 Hill St
Green Lake, WI 54941
events.venue_name:
Town Square Community Center MakerSpace
492 Hill St
Green Lake, WI 54941
events.description: Date: Saturday, June 27 Time: 10:30 am – 12:30 pm Location: MakerSpace Ages: 13 and up Capacity: 5–15 participants Join artist Susan Melgaard for a fun and creative fused glass workshop where you’ll design and create your own unique glass pieces. This hands-on class is perfect for beginners and experienced makers alike—Susan will provide guidance, ideas, and support as you bring your vision to life. Project Options: Option 1: Candle Holders / Cups Create beautiful and whimsical glass pieces that can be used for candles, jewelry, hair accessories, or other small items. They also make great gifts! Make 2 or 4. Option 2: Sun Catchers / Display Pieces Design stunning glass pieces to display on a table, bookcase, wall, or window. (Make one of each, two of the same, or two of each design) Choose your style: Four Seasons Trees Flower Meadows Pricing $50 for 2 pieces $80 for 4 pieces Additional Details All materials, tools, instruction, and creative guidance are included in the cost Please wear closed-toe shoes for safety Glass pieces will be fired twice at Susan’s studio; finished items will be ready in 1–2 weeks If you need your pieces sooner, please note this at registration and we will do our best to accommodate Participants may choose either date or attend both, and select one project per session.

events.title: 3rd Annual Par for the Playground
events.location: Tuscumbia Golf Course, Green Lake
events.venue_name: Tuscumbia Golf Course, Green Lake
events.description: Par for the Playground is an annual community golf outing benefiting Green Lake Parks & Recreation improvements. Originally created to support the successful Playground Park Revamp project, the event now continues to help fund future park enhancements and community gathering spaces throughout Green Lake. Saturday, June 27, 2026 Tuscumbia Golf Course – Green Lake, WI 9-Hole Scramble | 5:00 PM Shotgun Start This year’s proceeds will support the addition of a new shelter and picnic gathering space adjacent to Playground Park — creating a welcoming area for families, visitors, and community members to gather and enjoy the park together. $90 per golfer (includes golf, cart & dinner) Dinner-only option available for $30 Hole sponsorships available – $150 Raffles, drinks, games & community fun to follow Whether you golf, attend dinner, sponsor a hole, donate a raffle item, or simply come enjoy the evening, all proceeds go directly back into Green Lake parks and recreation spaces.

events.title: Yoga in the Vineyard w/Lotus Root Yoga
events.location:
Vines & Rushes Winery
410 County Road E
Ripon, WI 54971
events.venue_name:
Vines & Rushes Winery
410 County Road E
Ripon, WI 54971
events.description: Join us for Yoga in the Vineyard! Our popular weekly yoga class, with Lotus Root Yoga, held outdoors among the grapevines is coming back this summer! This gentle Vinyasa flow class is suitable for all levels. Be sure to bring your yoga mat, sunglasses, and plenty of water. The cost is $10 per class (option to register and pay online) Link to register online : https://clients.mindbodyonline.com/classic/ws?studioid=640759&stype=-7&sTG=23&sView=day&sLoc=0&date=06/18/26 Each class is led by a rotating team of instructors. Yoga goes from 5:30pm - 6:30pm. In the case of inclement weather, class will be cancelled. The winery is open until 9PM, with a selection of beverages, snacks, pizza (served until 8PM), and other food options. (outside food and drinks are not permitted) We can't wait to see you at this relaxing outdoor series every Thursday until the end of August!

events.title: Coffee Connect: First Fridays with the GLA
events.location: GLA Conference Room at the Town Square (2nd floor)
events.venue_name: GLA Conference Room at the Town Square (2nd floor)
events.description: Start your month with coffee, community, and conversations that care for Green Lake. Join the Green Lake Association on the first Friday of every month from 7:30-9:00 AM for Coffee Connect. A casual morning gathering open to anyone who loves the lake and the community around it. Whether you're interested in volunteering, learning more about the work happening across the watershed, connecting with fellow lake lovers, or simply stopping in for a cup of coffee and good conversation, there's always a seat at the table. Come by, stay awhile, and start your Friday with community and clean water in mind.

events.title: Art MidWisconsin: Hands on Art: Exploring the Human Form
events.location:
Town Square Community Center MakerSpace
492 Hill St
Green Lake, WI 54941
events.venue_name:
Town Square Community Center MakerSpace
492 Hill St
Green Lake, WI 54941
events.description: Dates: July 7 & 9 Time: 9 am - Noon Instructor: Travis Nygard Location: MakerSpace Level: Beginning to Intermediate Cost: $120 Note: This includes a $25 material fee Artists have been fascinated by other people since the Stone Age, and in this class, we will use depictions of the human body as a case study. It is for people of all experience levels and combines interactive lectures, hands-on exercises, and art-making. You can expect to see examples of great paintings, drawings, and sculptures, learn how artists have represented the human face in many ways. You will learn how to draw a face with accurate proportions. You will also learn how artists thought about the body in layers from the inside out—from the skeleton, to muscles, to skin, to clothing. We will even consider how artists have used color and layers to depict people in both realistic and wildly unrealistic ways. The course is geared toward adults, but mature high-school-age students are also welcome to enroll. The course includes some artistic representations of unclothed bodies. No prior knowledge or artistic skill is required to enjoy this class. Art supplies will be provided, including a set of watercolor paints and oil pastels for you to keep.

events.location:

events.venue_name:

events.description: Acrylics Made Fun with an Exploration into Abstract Art Dates: July 13, 14 & 15 Time: 9 am - 1 pm Instructor: Anne Gary Location: MakerSpace Level: Beginning to Intermediate Cost: $150 Note: A supply list will be provided upon registration Get ready to roll up your sleeves, splash some color around, and have a seriously fun time in this 4-hour acrylic painting class! Whether you’re a total beginner or someone who just hasn’t picked up a brush in a while, this session is all about letting your creativity loose in a relaxed, no-pressure atmosphere. We’ll kick things off with a quick intro to materials (don’t worry—nothing too technical), then dive right into playful demos where you’ll learn cool tricks like blending colors, creating texture, and bringing your painting to life layer by layer. Think of it as part lesson, part creative playground. As you paint, you’ll get plenty of guidance, tips, and encouragement—plus the freedom to add your own personal twist. There’s time to experiment, make happy little accidents, and discover your own style along the way. By the end of the class, you’ll walk away with a finished painting you can proudly show off—and maybe even surprise yourself with what you created.

events.description: Intro to Digital Photography Date: July 15 Time: 9 am - Noon Instructor: Eileen Reilly Location: Studio B Level: Beginning to Intermediate Cost: $75 Note: DSLR cameras and Smartphones are welcome Whether you are picking up a camera for the first time or looking to sharpen your foundational skills, this course offers a welcoming and practical starting point for your photography journey. This 3 hour course us designed for beginners who want to move beyond automatic mode and start taking intentional and compelling photos. Students will learn to navigate their camera’s settings-specifically the exposure triangle: aperture, shutter speed and ISO. The principles of composition will be covered by exploring the rule of thirds, leading lines and framing to build more visually engaging images. There will be a brief overview of lighting, especially natural light. The course will finish with an outdoor shoot, where students will apply everything they have learned into a real-world environment.

The course includes a calm and protected lake swim, rolling hills on the bike, and run along the lake making this triathlon perfect for beginners to veterans. Now heading into our 26th year, we continue to provide a fun race for all.

events.description: Intro to Watercolor Date: July 21 Time: 9 am - Noon Instructor: Carolyn Myers Location: MakerSpace Level: Beginning to Intermediate Cost: $75 Note: Paper will be provided for you, however a recommended supply list will be provided upon registration for personal purchase The wondered if watercolor is for you, but don’t know where to start? Or you have dabbled in watercolor, but it just isn’t making sense? There is a beauty an ease to watercolor when you learn the basics of techniques and materials. In this one session workshop we will do some exploration and experimentation with a variety of materials. We will explore both abstract and landscape exercises and engage in hands on projects. Participants will take home a painting they create as well as techniques to use in their future endeavors.

events.location:
Town Square Community Center Ballroom & Jury Bar
492 Hill St
Green Lake, WI 54941
events.venue_name:

492 Hill St
Green Lake, WI 54941
events.description: An Evening of Magic with Dennis Watkins Friday, July 24 Two Shows: 5:30 pm & 8:00 pm Town Square Ballroom & Jury Bar | Green Lake, WI Prepare to be amazed when award-winning magician and mentalist Dennis Watkins brings an unforgettable evening of magic to Town Square. A third-generation magician known for his critically acclaimed show The Magic Parlour in Chicago, Watkins delivers a dazzling mix of sleight-of-hand, mind-reading, storytelling, and humor that keeps audiences on the edge of their seats. This intimate live performance puts the magic right in front of you—sometimes even in your own hands—as impossible moments unfold just inches away. Watkins’ performances blend classic magic with modern wit, creating an interactive experience that will leave you laughing, questioning reality, and rediscovering a sense of childlike wonder. Whether you’re a longtime fan of magic or simply looking for a unique night out, this is a show you won’t want to miss.

events.description: Daniel Van Kirk's comedy is a unifying force. His live performances are a testament to why he's recognized as a "Comic To Watch" by Comedy Central. events.description: Join the Green Lake Association for an extraordinary evening of community, connection, and celebration at The Lake Ball, presented by Craig & Katie Culver. This signature gala brings together friends, neighbors, and partners who care deeply about Green Lake for a cocktail hour, elegant dinner, and spirited live auction—all in support of the lake we love and the work ahead. Seating is limited, so please reserve your seats online today at https://www.greenlakeassociation.org/lakeball2026. When the final paddle is raised, the celebration continues at The After Party at Waves Pub & Grill. Enjoy a complimentary bar, live music, and dancing as we keep the energy going well into the night. Tickets for The After Party are included for Lake Ball guests, or are available separately for $25 for those who’d like to join the festivities after the gala.
